rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek: hardcoded PEFT-name-to-module fixes should be driven from a single source of truth like stacked_params_mapping rather than ad hoc lists) is conceptually echoed by X's comment 1 (reuse pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded component list, noting drift across manager.py/offloader/registry.py) — a different concrete mechanism but the same underlying worry. Y never raises this; its findings (missing unet activation round-trip test, test duplication) are valid and well-grounded but orthogonal to what actual reviewers flagged, so its recall is much lower. Both are well-grounded and specific with file:line citations, giving comparable precision and actionability.
